text:
There once was a kid named Jack. Jack loved to climb trees. He climbed the tallest oak tree he could find when he was just eleven.

That year Jack learned something incredible. As he looked down through the branches, he saw a fox watching him. The fox spoke.

"Thank you for showing me the forest."

Jack didn't speak.

"You're a good student."

Jack didn't reply.

"You oughtta enjoy life."

Jack didn't smile.

That night Jack dreamed about the fox.

He climbed into an owl's nest. The owls roared. It turned into a tree.

Jack flew down. The fox greeted him warmly.

They sat together watching sunset.

The owl shrieked.

They flew away.

They met again by owl twilight moons. Years later.

Jack was an eagle.

The fox flew alongside a skyscraper.

They talked quietly.

Jack wondered if he missed climbing trees. Maybe not.

They flew onto a bridge.

They looked over oceans.

Maybe next owl moon.
